summary: Completed comprehensive backend testing of EduQuest LMS. 35/35 pytest tests passed covering auth (register/login/me via cookie & bearer/logout), courses (CRUD, enrollment, role guards), lessons, file upload & streaming, resources (link/file), activities (assignment/quiz with correct_index hidden from students), submissions (assignment + quiz auto-grade), grading with XP award, gamification (stats/badges/levels), and leaderboard with is_me + desc xp sort. All endpoints behaved as specified in the review request.

minor.issue: When teacher fetches a single activity, correct_index is correctly visible; but the endpoint is not listed in review spec — confirmed it works as expected and does not leak to students.
CORS_ORIGINS is set to '*' in backend/.env while allow_credentials=True. Browsers will reject credentialed requests with wildcard origin. Should use explicit origins for cookie auth from frontend.
No brute-force / rate limit lockout implemented (playbook recommends lockout after 5 failed attempts).
Cookie is set with secure=False; for preview/production over HTTPS it should be secure=True and samesite='none' to function cross-site with credentials.
No content-type / filename sanitization and no owner-restricted download; any authenticated or even unauthenticated caller can fetch any file by id via GET /api/files/{id} (no auth dependency).

action_items: Tighten CORS_ORIGINS to the actual frontend URL since allow_credentials=True (wildcard + credentials is invalid per CORS spec).
Add secure=True + samesite='none' to auth cookie for HTTPS preview/prod deployments.
Add auth requirement to GET /api/files/{file_id} or at least restrict to enrolled users / owner — currently it is fully public.
Consider adding brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (5 failed attempts) per auth playbook.
Seed admin upsert: current seed_admin only inserts if missing; if ADMIN_PASSWORD changes it will not update the existing doc.
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/files/{file_id} has no auth dependency — any file uploaded (possibly containing private submission content) is publicly downloadable by id.
CORS configured with '*' + allow_credentials=True is functionally broken for credentialed browser requests.
grade_submission awards quiz_master badge on assignments >=90%, which overloads the semantic (badge was intended for quizzes).
add_xp does not decrement level if xp is ever reduced; fine for current flows but worth noting.
Submissions collection lacks a unique (activity_id, student_id) index — relies on find_one check which is race-prone under concurrent submits.
test_credentials: admin@eduquest.com / admin123 (teacher, seeded). Test students created at runtime with prefix test_stu_<uuid>@example.com.
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates 2 test students + 1 course + lesson + resources + 1 assignment + 1 quiz + submissions; cleanup deletes the course cascade at end. Test students remain in DB (namespaced with uuid to avoid collisions).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_eduquest_api.py are fully self-contained and idempotent — reuse them as regression suite. Frontend not yet tested per request (testing_type: backend only).
